Class FormValidator

java.lang.Object
com.sait.workshop05.util.FormValidator
public final class FormValidator extends Object
Collects rules per control and shows inline error labels on blur or after the first submit. Each wrap call returns a VBox that contains the control and its error label so you add that VBox to the layout instead of the bare control. 
 FormValidator form = new FormValidator();
 VBox usernameField = form.field(tfUsername)
         .required("Username is required")
         .min(3, "At least 3 characters")
         .wrap();
 VBox pwField = form.field(pfPassword).required("Password is required").min(6, "Min 6 characters").wrap();
 VBox confirmField = form.field(pfConfirm).required("Please confirm password").wrap();
 form.match(pfPassword, pfConfirm, "Passwords do not match");
 if (!form.validateAll()) event.consume();

  • Method Details

    • field

    • choice

      public <T> FormValidator.ChoiceBuilder<T> choice(javafx.scene.control.ComboBox<T> combo)

    • match

      public FormValidator match(javafx.scene.control.TextInputControl a, javafx.scene.control.TextInputControl b, String message)
      Adds a cross-field rule so the confirm field must match the first field. Both fields revalidate when either changes.

    • validateAll

      public boolean validateAll()
      Validates every field, reveals all errors, returns true when all pass.
